def: adj. Of or belonging to Paradise.

v.n. = rūṅgṭe khaṛe honā, q.v.s.v. rūṅgṭā
wety: From Classical Persian روان (ravān, “fluid, flowing”), a compound of رو (rav) + ـان (-ân); the former is the present stem of رفتن (raftan).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Hairy, woolly, having bristles or down, &c. (see roʼāṅ).
n. f. S. रोदन weeping. Weeping and crying; lamentation.

(رو from रोदन To weep, and आशा Desire) s. f. Inclination to cry.

def: n. f. pl. of raīs . Gentry; the respectable residents of a place; big wigs. raī'sah , n. f. A princess; a lady of rank.

more: adj. Inclined or ready to cry.
(see رواس ro-ās ) adj. Inclined to cry, vexed, displeased, afflicted.

def: s. (dakh.) Weeping, crying, lamentation ( Harris ).

more: (or raﻌb ) s. m. Terrifying, fear, terrour, trembling with fear; awe.
subs: (-meṅ), To inspire with awe, to awe
(kisī-ke), To be terrified (by), to be overawed (by).

ety: fr. ruflan; rt. Zend rup = S. लुप्

more: part. act. (of رفتن ruftan ) in compos. Sweeping.

more: s. f. A fox. روباه بازي robāh-bāz-ī, s. f. Stratagem, wile, trick, cunning, artfulness, deceit, evasion, subterfuge.
subs: s.f. Foxiness, cunning, artfulness, trick, wile, stratagem, deceit, evasion, subterfuge

def: adj. & s.m. Pleasing, agreeable; giving an appetite, stomachic, tonic; — any medicine, or fruit, &c. which strengtheus the tone of the stomach and restores lost appetite, a stomachic, tonic, stimulant, carminative.
more: adj. A stomachick, something taken to excite appetite.
wety: From Sanskrit रोचक (rocaka).

def: adj. (f. -ā), & s.m. Enlightening, illuminating, irradiating; bright, splendid, beautiful; causing or exciting desire, pleasing, agreeable, pleasant, charming, lovely; sharpening or giving an appetite, stomachic, tonic; — a stomachic; — name of several plants; turmeric; — the firmament; the æther.
s.f. A charming or excellent woman; — a particular yellow pigment (com. called go-roćan, q.v.).
s.f. A particular yellow pigment ( = roćanā, or go-roćan, q.v.); — red arsenic, realgar.
s.m. = روده roda, q.v.
more: (for روده) s. m. 1. (A sinew used as) A bowstring. 2. A gut, catgut. 3. The branch of a creeper.

def: s.m. Weeping, wailing, lamentation; — a tear, tears.
more: s. m. Weeping, crying, a tear, tears.

def: s.m. Restraint, prohibition, stoppage, obstruction; a dam; bank, shore.

adj. & s.m. Obstructing, impeding, hindering; — obstructer, hinderer; the act of stopping, checking, restraining, preventing, impeding; oppressing; besieging, blockading; impediment, obstruction, obstacle, hindrance; confinement; blockade.
part. (of roʼīdan), Growing, &c. ( = ro, q.v.). part. Growing, germinating (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ḵẖẉud-ro, 'growing of itself').
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit रवि (ravi).

def: s.m. pl. of roʼāṅ, q.v.; — s.f. pl. of roṅ, q.v.
s.m. Sickness, disease, malady; infirmity, weakness; evil, worry, plague; fault, defect; difficulty, embarrassment; — the plant Costus speciosus or arabicus
more: n. m. S. रोग Pālī, rogo . 1. Sickness; disorder; disease. 1. Havā meṅ rog paidā ho gayā hai . 2. Roj kā ghar khāṅsī, laṛāī kā ghaṛ hāṅsī . Prov. The source of maladies a cough, the source of quarrels is a laugh. 3. Kuār karelā, Chait guṛ, Bhādoṅ mūlī khāë, Paisā kharche gāṅṭh kā, rog bisāhan jāë. Karelā in September, March guṛ , and August radish try, Your money you 'll just spend and with it but diseases buy. 4. Lagāyā rog javānī meṅ kyoṅ, miyāṅ, Jurat! Abhī to khel tamāshe ke the tuṁhāre din . Jurat. O Jurat! why in youth's fresh prime already love-sick grown? The season gay which sport and mirth and jollity should crown. 2. Epidemic; plague. 3. Botheration; plague; contention. 4. Defect; fault; failing; infirmity; a bad habit. 5. Difficulty; entanglement; embarrassment. Baṛe rog meṅ phaṅs gaë. rog bisānā, pālnā, yā lagā lenā , v. a. 1. To bring illness on oneself. 2. To acquire a bad …
subs: To contract disease, to bring illness (on oneself); to acquire a bad habit
s.f. 'Seat or place of sickness,' the body
s.m. 'Alleviator or curer of disease,' a physician
s.f. Cure or alleviation of disease
To put an end to an evil, or contention, &c., to settle a troublesome affair
adj. & s.m. Seized with sickness, sick, diseased; — one who is sick, an invalid
s.m. Sign or symptoms of a disease
adv. For the purpose of removing disease
(˚ga+ut˚), adj. & s.m. Engendering or occasioning disease; — a cause of disease
rog-har, adj. Taking away disease, remedial, medicinal, curative; — s.m. A medicine; — a physician.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit रोग (roga), from रुज् (ruj), from Proto-Indo-European *lewǵ-. First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi روگ (rvg /⁠rog⁠/, “sorrow”).

ety: rog, q.v. + Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Sick, sickly, diseased, ill, ailing, unwell; — sick person, invalid, valetudinarian
more: adj. Sick; ailing rogī , n. m. A sick person; an invalid.

ety: rogī+Prk. इल्लो or अलो = S. अ+लः
def: adj. & s.m. = rogī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Sick, sickly, diseased, ill, ailing, unwell; — sick person, invalid, valetudinarian
adj. & s.m. = rogī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Sick, sickly, diseased, ill, ailing, unwell; — sick person, invalid, valetudinarian

ety: rog, q.v. + Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Sick, sickly, diseased, ill, ailing, unwell; — sick person, invalid, valetudinarian
s.m. Name of a mountain in Ceylon, Adam's peak.

s.m. A rising, ascending, mounting riding on; growing, springing up; — semen virile; — a species of large forest-tree, Swietenia febrifuga; the sandal-tree; — name of a mountain (Adam's peak, in Ceylon); — name of an ancient Hindū king.
more: s. m. Name of a Rājā.

ety: rt. of ronā+haṭ = Prk. एअव्वट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Crying, weeping; — tears.
s.m. The Indian fig-tree (syn. baṛ) a kind of fragrant grass; — an epithet of the ninth Muhūrt, a part of the forenoon (extending to midday, in which Śrāddhas are especially to be observed).
n. f. 1. The 4th lunar mansion, comprising Aldebaran and four other stars in Taurus. 2. ( Myth .) The daughter of Rohan and wife of Chandra or the moon.

s.f. A red cow; a cow; — name of the fourth (or the ninth) Nakshatra or lunar mansion (personified as a daughter of Rohaṇ or of Daksh and the favourite wife of the Moon: it is figured by a wheeled carriage, and comprises Aldebaran and four other stars in Taurus); — lightning; — a girl in whom menstruation has just commenced; a woman stained with red pigments; — inflammatory affection of the throat; — Bengal madder; yellow myrobalan; — name of a wife of Vasudeva, and mother of Balarām.
more: s. f. 1. The name of the fourth mansion of the moon, comprising Aldebaran and four other stars in Taurus. 2. (in mythology) The wife of Chandra or the moon. 3. The daughter of Rohaṇ, wife of Vasudeva and mother of Balarāma.

def: s.m. = next, q.v.
s.m. A kind of deer; a species of fish ( = rohit, q.v.); — a kind of creeper; — a sort of deer said to resemble an ass.

adj. & s.m. Red, of a red colour; — the colour red; — a kind of deer; a species of fish, Cyprinus rohita, or C. denticulatus (com. called rohī, or rohū); — a kind of rainbow appearing in a straight form, Indra's bow unbent and invisible to mortals
subs: adj. & s.m. Having red horses; — the god of fire, Agni.

def: s.m.f. A kind of fish, Cyprinus rohita; — (f.) corner of the evelid.
more: s. f. The name of a fish. (Cyprinus denticulatus: Buch. ).

def: Sant. n. f. S. रोहित 1. A fish; Cyprinus rohita . 2. The corners of the eyelids. rohū kā paṭṭhā , n. m. The young of fish.
s.f. = roʼīn, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of roʼīdan; rt. Zend rud = S. रुध् or रुह्
def: part. Grown, sprung up (as a plant); produced.
s.f. Growth (of a plant); production; vegetation.
more: n. f. 1. The growth of a plant. 2. Vegetation.

def: (from رويدن To grow) s. f. Vegetation, growth of a plant.
s. m. Brass, bell-metal. روٴين تن roʼīn-tan, Brazen-bodied; a hero, an epithet of Isfandyār, whose body was impenetrable to weapons.
more: (plur. of روان roʼāṅ, q. v.) s. f. The hair on the arms, &c. bristle, pile, nap, down. روٴين بدلنا roʼeṅ-badalnā, To become sleek and fat (a horse), to change his coat.

s.m. A clerk whose business it is to keep the journal or diary of work done in an office
adv. Day and night, always, constantly (syn. din-rāt).

ety: S. रोकः 'buying with ready money'; and roka+Prk. डी and डओ = S. र and र+कः
def: s.f. Ready-money sale, cash transaction
s.f. Stop, restraint, check, restriction, prevention, prohibition, let, hindrance, obstacle, bar, barrier, interruption, stoppage; stay, support; screen, shelter (syn. āṛ)
more: n. f. S. रोध See آرَ (1—9). 1. Limitation; restriction. 2. Hindrance. 1. Mehrī kī rok, jān ke sok! Prov. A woman checked, your life sucked dry! 2. Āge rok, pīchhe ṭhok, sasur sarkā na jāë to kyā kare ? Prov. A block ahead driven from behind, if that father of a b—h wont move what am I do? rok thāṅb , n. f. 1. See āṛ . 4, 7. 2. Temporary remedy 3. Patching up a quarrel. rok-ṭok , n. f. 1. Obstruction. See rok . 2. Opposition; resistance; a sentry's challenge. Āj-kal shahar meṅ baṛī rok-ṭok hai. rok ṭok k. , v. a. 1. To challenge (as a sentry). 2. To take exception to. rok rakh'nā , v. a. 1. See bāz rakhnā , 1 to 6. 2. To impound; pound (cattle). rok rok-ke , adv. By little and little; sparingly. be-rok-ṭok , adv. Without let or hindrance.
adj. S. रोक buying with ready money. Ready money or cash.
(from رکنا roknā, q. v.) s. f. Prevention, prohibition, stop, restraint, let, hinderance. روک ٿوک rok ṭok, Let and hinderance, opposition, resistance.
subs: s.f. Restraint, check, stop, let, hindrance; stay, support, prop; temporary remedy, a patching up (as of a quarrel, &c.)
s.f. Let and hindrance, obstacle, obstruction, &c. ( = rok); opposition, resistance; challenging, calling in question, challenge (of a sentry)
v.t. To offer an obstacle to, to prevent, hinder; to oppose, resist; to challenge, to call in question, to take exception to.

def: s.f. Obstacle, ob struction, hindrance, &c. (see rok).

n. m. 1. Cash; ready money. 2. Specie, jewels, or valuables readily convertible into coin. 3. Stock; capital. 1. Ab to dūkān kī sārī rokaṛ mār baithe . 2. Oh ke rokaṛ bāṅdhe háṅḍe saë ? Rus. What! does he go about with all his cash about his person! (Said to a shopkeeper who defers making a payment on the plea that the other partner is out). rokaṛ bikrī , n. f. Ready-money sale; cash transaction. rokaṛ-bahī , n. f. Cash-book.

ety: S. रोकः 'buying with ready money'; and roka +Prk. डी and डओ = S. र and र+कः
def: s.f. Ready-money sale, cash transaction
more: s. f. ( रोक ) Ready money, cash.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀭𑁄𑀓𑁆𑀓 (*rokka) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀟- (-ḍa-), from Sanskrit रोक (roka, “buying with ready money”). Cognate with Punjabi ਰੋਕੜ (rokaṛ) / روکَڑ (rokaṛ), Sindhi روڪَڙِ (rokaṛi) / रोकड़ि, Gujarati રોકડ (rokaḍ), Marathi रोकड (rokaḍ).

def: s.m. (f. -nī, -in, or -inī), Cash-keeper, cashier, treasurer, banker.
more: s. m. ( रोक ) Ready money, cash.

def: n. m. A cashier; treasurer; cashkeeper.
s.m. = rosh, ros, qq.v. s.m. Anger, &c. (see ros)
subs: adj. Bearing, or feeling, or displaying wrath; wrathful, excitable, irascible, choleric.

subs: To change countenance, to become angry, be displeased; to change the direction of (one's) face, to turn away the face, to be inattentive; to turn away (from); to reject
(neut. of next, q.v.), v.n. 'The face to be turned away,' to be or become offended or displeased
(-se), To turn the face away (from), to become displeased, &c. ( = ruḵẖ badalnā, q.v.)

(-kī t̤araf), To keep the face (towards), to keep in regard, to continue friendship, maintain friendly intercourse (with).

ety: rok˚ = Prk. रुक्क(इ) or रुक्के(इ), fr. S. रुध् (acc. sing. neut. रुत्)+कृ
def: v.t. To stop, check, arrest, challenge (as a sentry), restrain, prohibit, hinder, impede, retard, prevent, obstruct, baulk, interrupt, intercept, interdict, forbid, suppress, oppose, resist, coerce; to hold, hold in or back, withhold, retain, keep, hold in detention, detain; to engage, secure, forestall, pre-engage; to avert, keep off, keep back, ward off, parry, guard; to preserve, protect, screen, cover; to shut close, block, fend, stop up, block up, fill up; to shut in, hem in, enclose, surround, invest, besiege; to bind (as by contract, &c.)
more: v. a. S. रोधन shut up, Pr. rundhaī, rumbhaī he hinders. See bāz rakhnā, thāmnā 1—4, and band k . 2—7. 1. To obstruct; block up; fence; hedge; wall in. 2. To impede; retard. 3. To intercept; break off; interrupt. 4. To catch (as a ball). 5. To challenge (as a sentry). Jāne-vāle sipahyā ke ke rokelā? Bhoj. Prov. Who can ever stop a soldier on his way? 6. To guard; ward off; parry. 7. To occupy, fill or take up (space). Jagah roknā . 8. To forestall; preengage; secure (by making advances). 9. To guard; preserve; protect. 10 To settle; appoint; afix. 11. To bind by contract; betroth. Laṛkā rok dīyā . 12. To hide; screen.
v. a. To enclose, surround, stop, block, fend, prevent, restrain, hinder, coerce, detain, interrupt, prohibit.
subs: v.t. To stop, restrain, detain, &c. ( = roknā); to impound, to pound (cattle).

ety: S. रोकः 'buying with ready money'; and roka +Prk. डी and डओ = S. र and र+कः
def: s.f. Ready-money sale, cash transaction
s.m. Preventer, hinderer, obstructer, &c.
s.f. Roll, register, catalogue; revenue roll (of the government)
more: n. m. 1. Roll; register; catalogue. 2. Rotation; turn. 3. The Govt. revenue. rol-dār , n. One who is entered on the Collector's roll or record.
subs: s.m. One who is entered on a roll (as on that of a Collector).

ety: S. लोलः, rt. लुल; see rolnā
def: s.m. Exercise (as of a horse), walk, turn, rotation (e.g. rol-ko le-jānā, 'to take (a horse) out for exercise, or for a turn).
s.m. Picked betel-nut (cf. rolan).
s.f. A kind of metre of four lines, each of 24 instants; with the last syllable long, and a pause at the eleventh instant
s.m. (Mārwārī), Rocky jungle.
n. A Hin. metre. It has four lines, each of 6+ (4+4) + 2=24 instants. The last syllable must be long, pause at 11th instant. Kellogg .
s.f. Anything picked out, the pick (of anything).
v.t. = رلانا rulānā, q.v. v.t. To cause (one) to roll, or plane, or smooth; to have (or get) rolled, or planed, &c.
more: (caus. of رونا ronā ) v. a. To cause to weep.

def: s.f. A mixture of rice, turmeric, and alum, with acid, used (by Hindūs) to paint the sectarial mark on the forehead; — a red insect which attacks standing wheat crops.
more: n. f. 1. A mixture of rice, turmeric, and alum, with acid, used to paint the forehead. 2. A red insect which attacks standing wheat crops.
s. f. A mixture of rice, turmerick, and alum, with acid, used to paint the forehead.

ety: rol˚ = Prk. लोल(इ) = S. लोल(ति), rt. लुल्
def: v.t. To roll; to plane, to smooth, to polish; to rub; to skim; — to separate (anything from the rubbish mixed with it by shaking or tossing it in a sort of fan), to sift, to winnow; to pick, pick out, select; to pick up, rake up; — to make (money) quickly or in abundance (e.g. ḵẖūb rupayā roltā hai); — rol-lenā, v.t. intens. of and = rolnā.
more: v. n. 1. To rake up; pick out; gather up. ki jiske khāk se letī thī khalq motī rol . Saudā In whose streets people picked pearls. 2. To winnow. 3. To sift or separate precious stones by rolling them or tossing them up and down in a sort of fan from the rubbish in which they may be found. 4. To skim; cream. 5. To earn, gain, or obtain abundantly. Khūb rupayā roltā hai . 6. To roll. 7. To plane; smooth; polish. 8. To rub; curry-comb. Ghoṛā aur phoṛā jitnā rolo vitnā hī baṛhe . Prov. A horse and a boil the more it's rubbed the more it grows (flourishes).

v. a. 1. To roll, to plane, to polish, to smooth. 2. To select, to pick. Saudā says: که جسکي خاک سي ليتي تهي خلق موتي رول Kijisḳī ḵẖāk se letī thī ḵẖalḳ motī rol, From the earth of which people picked out pearls.

def: prop. n. The city Rome
more: n. m. S. रोमन् Pālī, romam . The hair on the body of men and animals.

more: v. n. S. रोदन from रूद to weep, Pr. roanā, runnam wept, ruvaī he weeps. 1. To weep; cry; mourn; grieve over. 1. Ro de! banyā guṛ degā ? Cry cry away! the Banya will give you jaggery. Wont he? (To a child). 2. Rātoṅ roī, ek hi muā . Prov. Long nights so many wept for only one man dead. 3. Dhobī roë dhulāī ko, miyāṅ roëṅ kapṛoṅ ko . Prov. The washerman cries for his wages unpaid, the master for his clothes destroyed. 4. Laṛkā rove bāloṅ ko, nāī rove muṅḍvāī ko . Prov. The child cries o'er its shaven hair, the barber for his fee cries e'er. 5. Koṭhe-vālā rove, chhappar-vābā sove . Prov. In the brick-built house the master weeps, the inmate of thatched hut all soundly sleeps. (The brickhouse leaks). 6. Apne bāoloṅ roiye, aur ke bāoloṅ huṅsye . Prov. One's own idiot to tears shall move, another's will to laughter move. 7. Roë dān nahlṅ miltā . Prov. Begging is not getting. 8. Chor kī māṅ koth…

adj. 1. Crying; weeping. 1. Ronā dhonā! billī lāī āṭā, rone kā muṅh chāṭā . Boys' rhyme. Cry, baby, cry! the cat will flour apply, the crying face lick dry! 2. Unkī to nāk par ronā dharā rahtā hai . Upon his nose his tears stand ready to be shed. 2. Sad; melancholy. ronā pīṭnā , v. n. To bewail; lament. ronā dhonā , n. m. Weeping and wailing; affliction. ronī yā rotī sūrat, ronī shakal , n. f. A sad, melancholy, or sorrowful countenance. Is meṅ hī chor, ṭhag haiṅ, isī meṅ amol haiṅ, Is meṅ hī rotī shakl haīṅ, isī meṅ ṭhaṭhol haiṅ . The human body. Nazir The thief and murderer here dwell, here lives the soul as well, Here is the tearful eye, and here the mirthful jest doth lie. ro'nā , n. m. Distress ( dukhṛā , 2).

1. v. n. To cry, to weep. 2. To be displeased, to be melancholy. 3. adj. Addicted to crying. 4. s. m. Lamentation, grief. رودينا ro denā, 1. To cry, to weep. 2. To be displeased, to be filled with indignation. روني صورت ronī-ṣūrat, 1. adj. Of a sad countenance. 2. s. f. A melancholy or rueful countenance.
s. m. See H. روا rawā.

def: n. f. 1. Bread; meal-cake; a loaf. 1. Sir par jūtī, hā/?/h meṅ roṭi . Prov. The head shoe-beaten, in the hand a loaf. (Disgrace endured, a meal secured). 2. Gehūṅ kī roṭiyoṅ ko faulād kā peṭ chāhiye . Prov. For wheaten bread a stomach of steel. (Wheaten cakes, which poor men cant afford, are a sign of wealth, and it needs a strong mind to possess wealth without pride). 3. Roṭī par roṭī rakh-ke khā! Prov. Pile cakes on cakes and eat! (May plenty be your lot!) 4. Ham roṭī nahīṅ khāte, roṭī hameṅ khātī hai . Prov. The bread I eat each day consumes my life away. (She suffers at the hands of her supporter). 5. Khudā ke nām kī roṭī, Mohammd nām kā paisā, Alī ke nām kā khānā khilā, bābā! bhalā hogā! Mendicants. In God's name bread, and in Mohammed's pice bestow, In Ali's name a dinner, father, give! good know! 2. The scum or dregs of liquids. 3. Sustenance; maintenance; livelihood. Ghūṅgaṭ na jāno, dosto! tum zīnl dr ise, Is parde meṅ ye apnī kamātī haiṅ roṭiyāṅ . Nazīr. On no account, my friends, mistake it for a veil, I pray! It is a screen behind whioh she a living makes each day. 4. Feast; entertainment. Hin. 1. Woh bhī tīn roṭī degā . He too will give a three day's wedding feast. 2. Woh din kab hogā, gurū jā din dekhūṅ pūt? Bastī kī roṭī karāṅ jo ho pūt sa pūt! Play. Chaubolā . When, priest, will that day come to me when I a son may see? When I am with a good son blessed I'll feast the parish free. roṭī pakānā, yā panā ; Sant. piṭhā rapā , v. a. To cook; bake. roṭī peṭ bhar-kar milnā , v. n. To get a bellyful. Jahāṅ peṭ bhar-ke roṭī mili aur ītrāyā! No sooner he'd a belly full than he to swell and strut began. roṭī denā , v. a. 1. To give bread. 2. To feed; support; maintain. 3. To give a feast; entertain a party. roṭī kapṛā , n. m. Food and raiment ( roṭī , 2.). Uskā ye hāl hai ki ṛoṭī hai to kapṛā nahīn, kapṛā hai to roṭī nahīṅ . Wom. A sad state her's! if she gets bread clothes she must go without, if clothes she gets bread forced to go without. roṭī kapṛe, yā nān nafqe kā dāvā . A claim for maintenance on the part of a deserted wife. roṭī kapṛe kī khabar lenā, roṭī kapṛā d ., v. a. To feed and clothe; to maintain or support one. roṭī ko ronā , v. n. To want bread; to starve. Roṭī ko rove aur chūlhe pīchhe sove . Prov. For lack of bread she weeps, behind the fire place sleeps. roṭī khānā ; Sant. dākā jom , v. a. 1. To eat; take food. Māro to voh jī uṭhe, bin māre mar jāë, Kaheṅ pahelī Bīrbal, murdā āṭā khāë . Riddle. Ans. Pakhāvaj a drum. Strike it, and instantly it wakes to life, unstruck it's dead, Beerbal this riddle doth propound, 'the dead with flour is fed.' 2. To live or earn by; make a living out of. roṭiyoṅ kā mārā , adj. 1. Starved. 2. Famine-stricken. roṭiyāṅ toṛnā , Slang. v. n. To eat the bread of idleness; to loaf; sponge. roṭiyāṅ lagnā , v. n. ( Met .) To swell; look big. dāl roṭī se khush h . v. n. ( Met .) To be well off. khamīrī roṭī , n. f. Leavened bread.
s.m. A servant who receives bread or food in lieu of wages.
n. m. A domestic servant who gets food in lieu of wages. roṭī-wālā , n. m. A baker ( nānbāī ).

ety: Pehl. roj; Zend raoćaṅh, rt. ruć = S. रुच्; cf. S. रोचः
def: s.m. Day (syn. din); a day of twenty-four hours; — daily wages, hire or wages for a day; — adv. Daily, a day, per diem; every day
more: n. m. 1. Day ( din ). 2. Daily wages. Roz baṭe ānā. roz , adv. 1. A day; per day; per diem. Do āne roz milte haiṅ . 2. ( roz roz .) Every day; always. Roz kuāṅ khodnā, roz pānī pīnā . Prov. A well dig every day, then water drink you may. (Earning one's bread hard). roz-afzūṅ , adj. Increasing or progressing day by day. roz-baṭnā , v. n. To be distributed (daily wages). roz-ba-roz, roz-marrah , adv. 1. Day after day, 2. Constantly ( din-ba-din ). roz-i-jazā , n. The day of judgment. roz-raushan , Broad day; open day. roz-i-qayāmat, yā hashr , n. m. The day of resurrection. roz-marrah , adj. 1. Everyday; ordinary; customary; usual. 2. Conventional; idiomatic. roz-marrah , n. m. 1. Daily or everyday talk. 2. An idiom. Dehlī-vāloṅ kā roz-marrah achchhā hai . 3. Daily transactions or proceedings. roz-marrah hisāb . 1. Daily account; account current. 2. ( Arith .) Practice. roz-nāmchah, roz-nā…

(or roj ) s. m. A day. روز افزون roz-afzūn, Increasing daily. روز باعيش roz bāaﻌish, A good day (to you). روز بروز roz ba-roz, adv. def: s.m. Fasting; a fast; Lent (see ramaẓān); a fast-day
more: n. m. A fast; fasting; esp. the Mah. fast of 30 days in the month of Ramzān; a fast-day. Musalmīn uṭh nivāj, bandgī, rozā, bāṅg, dugānā hai, Subhe bhaī uṭh murgī mārī, bhisht kāhāṅ se jānā hai? Kabīr. The Moslem wakes and says his prayers of sorts and fasts, but lo! At early dawn a fowl he kills, to Heaven how can he go? roza-khor ; H. chartī , n. m. One who does not fast. rozah-dār ; Pop. roz-dār ; H. bartī , n. m. One who fasts or keeps Lent. rozah rakhnā , v. n. To keep a fast; to fast. rozah khānā , v. a. 1. ( rozah toṛnā ) To break a fast before the proper time. 2. Not to keep a fast. rozah kholnā , v. a. To break a fast ( iftār k .).
s. m. Fast, lent. روزه دار roza-dār, s. m. One who fasts or keeps lent. روزه رکهنا roza rakhnā, To keep a fast, to fast. روزه کهولنا roza kholnā, v. a. To break a fast.
subs: To break a fast before the proper time
s.m. One who avoids keeping a fast
s.m. One who fasts or keeps Lent
To keep a fast; to fast
To avoid keeping a fast; to break a fast before the proper time
To break a fast, to partake of food after keeping a fast.

To abscond; run away; conceal one's self; evade service of summons. rū-poshī , n. f. Absconding; hiding. Huā kyā fāëdū parde-nashīṅ ke dil lugāṅe se, Ki rū-poshī hameṅ karnī paṛī sāre zamane se . My heart set on a woman veiled, no good I've got, To hide my face from all the world is now my lot. rū riāyat , n. f. Partiality; countenance; favor. rū riāyat k . v. a. To countenance; favor. rū-siyāh , adj. Having one's face blackened; disgraced; infamous. rū-shinās , n. m. One who is known to another by sight; an acquaintance. rū-shanāsī , n. f. Knowing one by sight; acquaintanceship. rū-kash , n. m. A cover of a mirror. rū-gardāṅ , adj. Turning away the face or side; turning inside out. rū-gardāṅ k. , v. a. To turn the cloth of a coat inside out. rū-gardānī ,…

more: s. m. Weeping. See رودن rodan.

s.m. Blood.

s. m. The bed of a river, a river.
s.m. 'The Roarer, or Howler,' the god of tempests, (in the later mythology) an epithet of Śiva; an epithet of the inferior manifestations of Śiva (as the personified roaring of the storms)
more: s. m. A name or form of Siva.

s.f. The wife of Rudra, the goddess Durgā.
s.f. A kind of lute or guitar.
s.m. Face, &c. ( = rū, q.v.)

def: n. f. A Statement; proceeding; record in a case. The report of a subordinate officer deputed to inquire into any affair; the return to an official inquiry. rūedād-i-adālat . A judicial proceeding. rūedād-i-misl . The facts shewn in the records of a case. rūedād-i-muqaddama . The narrative or whole course of proceedings in a cause; the merits of a case.
s.m. pl. of rafīq, q.v.
more: plur. of رفيق rafīḳ, q. v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رُفَقا (rufaqā), from Arabic رُفَقَاء (rufaqāʔ).

def: s.f. Sweeping
subs: ruft-o-rob, s.m.? A clean sweep; — a broom.

def: v.n. To sweep, sweeping, brushing.
part. Broken; bent, curved, crooked; diseased, sick, infirm.
s.f. Brokenness; crookedness, curvedness; infirmity, sickness, disease.

ety: v.n. of روح 'to be entered, or be cooled, by the wind,' &c.
def: s.f. The soul, spirit, life, the vital principle, the breath of life; the spirit or essence (of anything); inspiration, divine revelation, the Qorʼān; prophecy, prophetic commission; — the angel Gabriel ( = rūḥuʼl-amīn)
more: n. f. روح a gentle wind, breath. Pl. arvāh . 1. Soul; spirit. 1. Jaisī rūh, vaise farishte . Prov. The augel as the soul. 2. Jumerāt ke din rūheṅ ātī haiṅ . On Thursday the spirits of the dead appear. 2. Essence ( sat ). Is davā kī rūh nikāl lo. rūh-afzā , adj. Exhilarating. rūh-ul-amīṅ , n. m. The angel Gabriel. rūh-ul-quds ; Sant. dhorom jive . The Holy spirit; the angel Gabriel. rūh-ul-lāh ; The spirit of God; Jesus Christ. rūh bhaṭaknā , v. n. 1. To haunt or hover (the spirit). 2. To hanker after. rūh kāṅpnā, yā tharrānā , v. n. 1. To shake or tremble with fear. 2. To abhor; detest. rūh nikālnā , v. a. 1. To extract the essence of a thing; to pick out the brains. 2. To kill; put to death. rūh nikalnā, rūh parvāz k. , v. n. 1. To take wing (the soul). 2. To be stunned or frightened to death.

(Heb. ) s. f. Soul, spirit. روح افزا rūḥ-afzā, Increasing the spirit or life. روح الامين rūḥu-l-amīn, The angel Gabriel. روح القدس rūḥu-l-ḳuds, The holy spirit, the angel Gabriel. روح الله rūḥu-l-lāh, The spirit of God, Jesus Christ. (Plur. ارواح).
subs: adj. Prolonging life; increasing the spirits, exhilarating
s.m. 'The trusty or faithful spirit,' the angel Gabriel
or rūḥuʼl-quds, The angel Gabriel; (with Christians) the Holy Spirit
The angel Gabriel; Jesus Christ; (with Christians) the Spirit of God, the Holy Ghost
The spirit to take flight, to die
adj. Nourishing or enlivening the soul
Someone's soul was thirsty (a phrase used by women when a pitcher or vessel full of water breaks of itself)
(-kī), To take the life (of), to kill; to extract the essence (of a thing)
(-kī), The soul or life to quit (the body), life to depart, to die; to be frightened to death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian روح (rūḥ), from Arabic رُوح (rūḥ, “spirit”).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Soiled, used; old.
adj. Used; old. Rūhā bartan .
adj. Having a soul or spirit; spiritual; of or relating to the angels or the Jinn; — s.m. Spiritual being; an angel; a Jinn.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian روحانی (rūhānī), from Arabic رُوحَانِيّ (rūḥāniyy).

def: s.f. Spirituality.

ety: fr. P. rū; or corr. of A. rūyat, q.v.
def: s.f. Brightness, freshness, cheerfulness; beauty; — regard, attention to the wishes (of).
s.m. pl. (of rāhib), Monks, religious recluses.
s.f. (dialec.) = rohū, rohit, q.v. s.m.f. A kind of fish, Cyprinus rohita; — (f.) corner of the evelid.
s.f. Cotton (carded or cleaned), cotton-wool (when not separated from the seed, it is termed kapās); — the white ant, Termes bellicosus
more: n. f. S. रोम Cotton; carded cotton; cotton separated from the seed. In the seed it is called kapās , and in the field bāṛī. rūī-dār , adj. Quilted or stuffed with cotton. rūī dhunnā , v. a. To card cotton. rūī sā badan ; Tir. tūr sau deh , A smooth skin like cotton. rūī se hāth pāṅoṅ . Soft, tender limbs. rūī kā gālā , n. m. 1. A flock of cotton. 2. Any thing soft and smooth or white as cotton. rūī kī tarah tūm ḍālnā, rūī sā dhun ḍālnā , v. a. See dhajjī uṛānā .
subs: adj. Quilted with cotton, stuffed with cotton

def: (from روي rūʼī ) s. m. A seller of cotton.
adj. = روئيا rūʼiyā, q.v. s.m. See روئيا rūʼiyā.
s.m. See روئيا rūʼiyā.
s.m. A seller of cotton, a cotton-merchant ( = rūʼīhā).
more: n. m. A seller of cotton.

def: s.m. Pain, sickness, illness, disease ( = rog, q.v.).
s.m. A kind of insect very troublesome to birds.
more: s. m. The name of an insect very troublesome to birds. ¶ roj (for Pers. روز in dakh.) Day.

def: s.f. corr. of roz-gārī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Serving; earning; — one who earns.
part. adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Teased, or worried (esp. by the insect called rūj, as an animal, especially a bird); — an animal so teased.
more: adj. (An animal, or bird more particularly) much teased by the insect روج rūj.

ety: rujh˚ = Prk. रुज्झ(इ) = S. रुध्य(ते), pass. of rt. रुध्
def: v.n. To be stopped, be obstructed, be shut or closed, be blocked up (cf. rupnā; ruknā); — to be oppressed, or harassed, or teased, or worried
more: v. n. To be stopped, bunged, or filled up. 1. Chhek rujh gayā . 2. Kān rujh gayā .

def: v.t. To oppress, harass, tease, worry, &c.

r: rujūʻ, rujūʻ karnā, se, kī t̤araf, rujūʻ lānā, meṅ, rujūʻ honā
def: v.n. To be returned; to be referred; — to turn (to, or towards, kī t̤araf); to incline (to).
more: n. f. رجع returning. 1. Turning towards; inclination; bent; leaning. 2. Reference; recourse; appeal. rujū k., yā lānā , v. a. 1. To turn towards; incline to. 2. To repair or have recourse to. 3. To present; lay before; bring into court; institute; refer, appeal. rujū karvānā , v. a. To bring about. rujū h. , v. n. To incline to; turn towards.

(from رجع) s. m. f. 1. Return, recourse, turning towards, bent, bias, appearing. 2. Reference, appeal. رجوع کرنا rujūﻌ karnā, To return, refer, allude, appeal, have recourse to, repair to. رجوع لانا rujūﻌ lānā, To turn towards, to betake oneʼs self, to side with, to submit, to yield.

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of rūkh, q.v. s.m. Face, countenance; cheek; face, aspect; point, side quarter, direction; — the rook or castle (at chess; cf. rath)
subs: To change countenance, to become angry, be displeased; to change the direction of (one's) face, to turn away the face, to be inattentive; to turn away (from); to reject
(neut. of next, q.v.), v.n. 'The face to be turned away,' to be or become offended or displeased
(-se), To turn the face away (from), to become displeased, &c. ( = ruḵẖ badalnā, q.v.)

(-ko), ruḵẖ karnā (-kī t̤araf), To turn the face (to or towards), to proceed (towards); to lean or incline (to); to countenance, to encourage, to familiarize; to attend (to), pay attention (to), to accede (to)
(-kī t̤araf), To keep the face (towards), to keep in regard, to continue friendship, maintain friendly intercourse (with).

def: s.f. = rūkhan, q.v.; — s.m. corr. of rūkh, q.v. s.f. (m.?), A small quantity or measure over and above that purchased which is given or taken gratis, a small quantity thrown in over and above; — adv. Given into the bargain, over and above, to boot (syn. ghaluʼā; seṅt).
s.m. Stoppage, hindrance ( = rok); — 2˚ (past conj. part. of ruknā, q.v.), adv. = ruk-kar, q.v.
more: s. (dakh.) A tree: see روکهـ rūkh,

def: n. m. Hallooing; calling out. rūkā denā , v. n. To halloo; call out; cry out; exclaim. 1. Mānas jātag bhūke marte, roëṅ tan dekar rūkā . W. Rus. song. The master famished with his children all cry out and call on you! 2. Būḍhā jāṭ mare bhūkā, parā paras māṅ de rūkā . Rus. Prov. An old Jaṭ dying of hunger on the public platform calls aloud.

ety: for A. رقعة, v.n. fr. رقع 'to patch or piece,' &c.
def: s.m. A bit, piece, a scrap (of paper, &c.); a note, epistle, letter, billet (syn. ćiṭṭhī); an invitation; a receipt
more: n. m. رقع he patched it. 1. See chiṭṭhī . 2. A receipt. 3. An invitation. ruqqa likhne-wālā ; H. hunḍī-bhejā . The drawer of a cheque. hāmil-i-ruqqa , n. m. The bearer of a cheque or note.
(from رقع To repair a garment, to piece: Heb. To extend, to expand) s. m. 1. A bit, a piece. 2. A note, epistle, letter, billet. رقعه وار ruḳʼaﻌ-wār, Paper fit for writing letters on.
subs: s.m. Paper fit for writing letters on, letter-paper.

def: s.m. pl. = ruqʻāt, q.v. s.m. pl. (of رقعه ruqʻa), Notes; a collection of epistles, a letter-book.

s.f. = روکهن rūkhan, q.v. s.f. (m.?), A small quantity or measure over and above that purchased which is given or taken gratis, a small quantity thrown in over and above; — adv. Given into the bargain, over and above, to boot (syn. ghaluʼā; seṅt).
more: n. f. Given in the bargain; over and above.
s. f. To boot. See روک rūk.

def: s.f. Stopping, hindering, preventing.
v.t. To cause to enclose, to surround; to hinder, to stop.
more: (caus. of روکنا roknā, q. v.) v. a. To cause to enclose, surround, stop, hinder.

ety: rukā(nā) + āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं — and ˚āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.m. Resistance (in Mechanics).
more: n. m. 1. Hindrance. 2. Prevention. 3. Detention. 4. Stagnancy; stagnation. Rukāo khūb nahīṅ taba kī ravānī meṅ, Ki bū fasād kī ātī hai band pānī meṅ . Zauq. The flow of passion warm to check's not well, From stagnant water comes a fetid smell. 5. Hesitation; reserve. 6. Misunderstanding; coolness. rukāo-zor , ( Mech .) Resistance. rukāo kī davā , See imsāk .
n. m. 1. See rok , and imsāk . 2. A hitch; difference; misunderstanding.
(from رکانا or روکنا q. v.) s. m. Hinderance, prevention, detention.

def: s.m. pl. (of رقعه ruqʻa), Notes; a collection of epistles, a letter-book.
more: n. Pl. of رقعه 1. Notes. 2. A collection of letters; a letter book.
s. plur. (of رقعه) 1. Notes. 2. A collection of epistles.

ety: rukā(nā) + āʼo = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं — and ˚āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.m. Resistance (in Mechanics).
more: s. f. Prevention, hinderance, detention, backwardness.

ety: prob. Zend rukhsh = S. ruć; but cf. rū, rūʼe
def: s.m. Face, countenance; cheek; face, aspect; point, side quarter, direction; — the rook or castle (at chess; cf. rath)
more: n. m. 1. Face; aspcct (اور 1.). 1. Is rukh baiṭho . 2. Ab to rukh bhī nahīṅ karte . 2. ( In comp .) The cheek, as rukh-sār . 3. Side. 4. The castle (chess). rukh badalnā, yā phernā , v. a. To turn away from; to discountenance. rukh phirnā , v. n. To be offended or displeased. rukh k., yā denā , v. a. 1. To turn the face or proceed towards; to lean to. 2. To countenance; encourage. rukh na k. , v. a. Not to pay attention to; to disregard.

s. m. 1. The cheek. 2. The castle at chess. 3. Face, point, side, quarter. رخ پهيرنا يا بدلنا ruḵẖ-pherna or -badalna, 1. To be inattentive. 2. To reject. 3. To turn away from. 4. To be angry, to be displeased. رخ دينا ruḵẖ denā, To familiarize. رخ رکهنا ruḵẖ rakhnā, To continue friendship, to maintain friendly intercourse. رخ کرنا ruḵẖ karnā, To turn the face (towards), to proceed (towards); to attend to, to accede to.
subs: To change countenance, to become angry, be displeased; to change the direction of (one's) face, to turn away the face, to be inattentive; to turn away (from); to reject

(-se), To turn the face away (from), to become displeased, &c. ( = ruḵẖ badalnā, q.v.)

(-ko), ruḵẖ karnā (-kī t̤araf), To turn the face (to or towards), to proceed (towards); to lean or incline (to); to countenance, to encourage, to familiarize; to attend (to), pay attention (to), to accede (to)
(-kī t̤araf), To keep the face (towards), to keep in regard, to continue friendship, maintain friendly intercourse (with).
ex: to change countenance; to become angry or displeased
to turn away; to change the direction of one's face; to reject; to be inattentive
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رخ (rux).

def: s.m. A tree (syn. peṛ)

n. m. S. वृक्ष a tree; Pr. vachchho or rukkha ; Pālī, ruḳkho . 1. A tree. 1. Rūkh binā nā nagrī sohe, bin bargan nā kaṛyāṅ; Pūt binā nā mātā sohe, lakh sone meṅ jaṛyāṅ . Towns bare of trees please not the eye, nor beams please boards without, No matron's bright without a son, with jewels though decked out. 2. Inke chāṭe rākh bhī nahīṅ rahe . Where he settles (locust-like) not a leaf is left. 2. E. A sugar cane. rūkh-chaṛhā , Wom. n. m. lit. A climber of trees. A monkey.
more: 1. s. m. A tree. روکهـ چڙها rūkh-chaṛhā (q. d. A climber of trees) s. m. A monkey. 2. adj. Plain, unseasoned.

ety: Prk. रुक्खा and रुक्खओ; S. रूक्षः and रूक्षकः
def: adj. (f.-ī), Dry, plain (as bread, food, &c.), simple, pure, unseasoned, unsavoury, insipid; dry, vapid, flat, tame; plain, blunt, harsh; unaffecting, cold, jejune; rough, unkind, unfriendly, displeased, cross, cool, indifferent
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Dry, plain, blunt, harsh, &c. (words, or manner, &c.); — plain or simple food, humble fare.

ety: Prk. रुक्खा and रुक्खओ; S. रूक्षः and रूक्षकः
def: adj. (f.-ī), Dry, plain (as bread, food, &c.), simple, pure, unseasoned, unsavoury, insipid; dry, vapid, flat, tame; plain, blunt, harsh; unaffecting, cold, jejune; rough, unkind, unfriendly, displeased, cross, cool, indifferent
more: adj. S. रुक्ष; Pālī, rukkho . 1. Dry; plain; insipid; vapid; unseasoned; not buttered or spiced. 1. Rūkhī roṭī na khāo, ṭake ser ām! Delhi Street cry. Why eat dry bread? a seer of mangoes for two pice I sell! (with each bit of dry bread the poor man takes a bite from a mango to make it go down). 2. Telī khasam kīyā, aur rūkhī khāī! Prov. Marry an oilman and live on dry bread! 3. Ghī gir paṛā, mujhe rūkhī bhātī hai . Prov. My butter spilt, dry rice doth please me well. 4. Rūkhā khānā, dhartī sonā Nāṅh suhelā phakkar honā . Rus. Prov. On dry bread live, on bare ground lie, the ascetic's life is hard to try. 2. Dry; cold; cool; indifferent; lukewarm; apathetic; formal; tame; flat. rūkhā sūkhā . adj. Plain; dry. rukhā sūkhā , n. m. Potluck; humble fare; poor meal. Rūkhī sūkhī jo Allāh detā hai woh khā letā hai rūkhā-pan, rukhāī , n. m. 1. Dryness; roughness. 2. Indifference; coolness, etc. r…
adj. Dry, plain, rough, harsh, unkind, unfriendly, pure, simple, unseasoned. روکها سوکها rūkhā-sūkhā, adj. Plain, blunt, harsh (words, &c.).
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Dry, plain, blunt, harsh, &c. (words, or manner, &c.); — plain or simple food, humble fare.

def: (from روکها) s. f. 1. Plainness, dryness. 2. Unkind looks, indifference.
s. f. Roughness, plainness, dryness, unfriendliness.

s.f. = رکهائي rukhāʼī, q.v. s.f. Roughness, dryness (as of bread, &c.), plainness, staleness, unseasonedness, insipidity; indifference, coolness, coldness, incivility, harshness, unfriendliness, unkind looks.

ety: rūkhā, q.v.+āwaṭ = Prk. वट्टी = S. वृत्तिः; — and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Roughness, dryness (as of bread, &c.), plainness, staleness, unseasonedness, insipidity; indifference, coolness, coldness, incivility, harshness, unfriendliness, unkind looks.
more: n. f. S. रुक्ष 1. Roughness; dryness. 2. Indifference; coolness; coldness. Jab /?/gāvaṭ voh merī har bāt meṅ pāne lagā Bātoṅ bātoṅ meṅ rukhāī yār batlāne lagā . Gazanfar. When in my words my love for her she saw The more with every word she used to thaw. rukhāī kī lenā, rukhāī denā , v. n. To be cold or indifferent.

def: s.m. Alabaster; marble.

s. m. (dakh.) See رخ ruḵẖ.

ety: for A. رخصة v.n. fr. رخص 'to be soft or tender'
def: s.f. (orig.) 'Facilitation, license, indulgence'; leave, permission to depart, leave of absence, dismission, congé; dismissal, discharge
more: n. f. رخص gave permission. See چهتَي 1,3. rukhsat-i-ittifāqī , Casual leave. rukhsat-i-riāyatī , Privilege leave. rukhsat-i-bilā vaza tankhuāh , Leave on full pay. rukhsat denā , v. a. See chhuṭṭī denā. Ab jīne ko tum rukhsat do, aur marne ko mehmān Khairāt karo, ehsān karo, yā pun karo, yā dān karo . On old age. Nazīr. Bid life adieu, and welcome death your guest to be, Give alms, do good, and gifts bestow in charity. rukhsat ba-vaza tankhuāh . Leave without pay. rukhsat k. , v. a. 1. To permit to depart; give leave to go ( bidā k .). 2. To dismiss. rukhsat milnā , v. n. See chhuṭṭī milnā , 1. rukhsat h . v. n. 1. To take leave; depart. 2. To depart this life.

(from رخص Was cheap, easy, remiss. Chald. Hoped, trusted) s. f. Leave, license, congé, dismission, discharge, permission, indulgence. رخصت دينا ruḵẖṣat denā, To permit, to dismiss, to give leave to go. رخصت طلب ruḵẖṣat-t̤alab, Asking leave to depart. رخصت کرنا ruḵẖṣat-karnā, To permit, to give leave, to dismiss. رخصت هونا ruḵẖṣat honā, To depart, to have leave to depart.
subs: (-ko), To give (one) leave to go, to dismiss; to grant leave or license, to permit ( = ćhuṭṭī denā)
v.t. To permit or suffer to depart, give (one) leave to go, to bid adieu to, to dismiss; to send away, discharge
To ask leave; to request or beg for indulgence or delay
v.n. To take leave, to have leave to depart, to depart, bid adieu (to, -se).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian رُخْصَت (ruxsat), from Arabic رُخْصَة (ruḵṣa).
